plan.goal: Implement the protected repo .prism state signatures design from docs/PROTECTED_PRISM_STATE_SIGNATURES.md, covering signed protected streams, trust-bundle verification, fail-closed hydration, protected write-path enforcement, migration semantics, and validation coverage.
plan.title: Implement the protected repo .prism state signatures design from docs/PROTECTED_PRISM_STATE_SIGNATURES.md, covering signed protected streams, trust-bundle verification, fail-closed hydration, protected write-path enforcement, migration semantics, and validation coverage.

acceptance.label: Call sites for repo event append/load and published-plan sync are identified.
Existing tests that currently accept external edits or legacy unsigned hydration are identified as migration targets.

node.summary: Map the existing unsigned JSONL append/load paths, current authenticated mutation seams, and behavior-changing tests that will need migration or replacement under the protected-state design.
node.title: Inventory current unsigned repo publication and hydration paths

acceptance.label: One protected-state module owns stream ids, verification statuses, envelope/payload types, and canonical serialization rules.
The design keeps `lib.rs` as a facade and places substantive logic in dedicated submodules.
Protected authoritative scope is fixed to the spec’s v1 stream set and does not silently expand.

node.summary: Introduce the shared protected-state subsystem that freezes the v1 protected stream set, verification statuses, canonical JCS envelope schema, hash/signature rules, predecessor linkage, and stream-family abstractions used by all protected repo `.prism` logs.
node.title: Define the protected-state foundation and canonical envelope model

acceptance.label: Runtime authority ids, runtime key ids, trust bundle ids, public verification metadata, and revocation semantics exist outside repo `.prism`.
The active runtime key for new writes is selected from non-revoked trust state.
Portable trust-bundle import/export flows exist for fresh-machine verification.

node.summary: Continuing protected-state implementation; investigating final prism-mcp runtime freshness regression before full-workspace validation passes.
node.title: Extend the shared-runtime trust plane with signing keys and trust bundles

acceptance.label: Protected streams are verified as `Verified`, `LegacyUnsigned`, `UnknownTrust`, `Tampered`, `Corrupt`, `Truncated`, `Conflict`, or `MigrationRequired`.
Append rejects orphan tails, duplicate ids, bad predecessor hashes, and non-verified stream states.
Legacy migration emits a signed `LegacyImported` baseline and archives unsigned bytes outside repo `.prism`.

node.summary: Implement canonical JCS hashing/signing, Ed25519 envelope emission, append-only predecessor-hash chaining, stream verification/classification, conflict detection, truncation handling, and deterministic legacy-baseline migration primitives in a reusable core engine.
node.title: Build the shared protected-stream append and verify engine

acceptance.label: Repo-scoped concept, contract, memory, and concept-relation publication uses signed protected events instead of raw filesystem edits as authority.
Hydration of these streams is fail-closed and only projects verified data into hot state.
Derived docs or convenience artifacts remain regenerated outputs, not authoritative mutation inputs.

node.summary: Move contracts, concepts, memory, and concept-relations off direct `serde_json` line append/load paths and onto the shared protected-stream engine, following the recommended rollout from a simple stream family to the rest of the knowledge logs.
node.title: Migrate repo knowledge streams onto the protected engine

acceptance.label: Per-plan streams become the only authoritative repo plan truth in v1.
Index and active/archived placement are derived from verified streams, not mutation inputs.
Conflict and reconcile semantics are explicit and prevent silent continuation on diverged valid heads.

node.summary: Replace `.prism/plans/index.jsonl` plus `active/` and `archived/` authority with signed per-plan streams under `.prism/plans/streams/*.jsonl`, keep plan lifecycle truth in signed plan events, and regenerate index/placement artifacts as derived projections.
node.title: Rework published plan persistence into signed per-plan streams with derived indexes

acceptance.label: Authoritative writes refuse `Conflict`, `Corrupt`, `Truncated`, `Tampered`, `UnknownTrust`, `LegacyUnsigned`, and `MigrationRequired` streams as required by the spec.
Hydration only projects verified protected data into hot memory and clearly labels diagnostic-only access to raw invalid content.
Watcher loop suppression uses recent self-write matching and never treats filesystem observation as identity proof.

node.summary: Thread protected-stream verification through session hydration, mutation admission, refresh/watch handling, and hot-memory projection so external edits are classified as `self-write` or `external_or_unknown`, invalid streams stop authoritative reads/writes, and watcher echoes are suppressed without inferring authorship from the filesystem.
node.title: Integrate verification gates into hydration, mutation, and watcher handling

acceptance.label: CLI surfaces exist for verification, migration, trust import/export, quarantine, repair, and reconcile flows listed in the spec.
Read and diagnostic surfaces include verification status, stream id, protected path, last verified event/hash, trust bundle id, diagnostic code/summary, and repair hint.
Normal hydration never auto-heals protected streams; repair and reconcile remain explicit operator actions.

node.summary: Add operator-facing command and MCP surfaces for verify, diagnose, migrate-sign, trust import/export, quarantine, repair-to-last-valid, and reconcile-stream, and surface per-stream verification metadata on read/diagnostic views.
node.title: Expose operator tooling and diagnostics for verification, migration, repair, and trust workflows

acceptance.label: Existing tests that allow unsigned legacy hydration or external authoritative edits are replaced with migration or failure-mode expectations.
Targeted tests cover canonical serialization bytes, signature verification, trust-bundle resolution, conflict detection, truncation, quarantine/repair, and self-write suppression.
After targeted validation passes, the full workspace test suite passes and the release MCP binaries are rebuilt and restarted if required by the changed surface.

node.summary: Replace now-obsolete green-path tests, add fixture-level canonical-byte and signature verification cases, cover migration/repair/conflict/tamper scenarios, run targeted tests for touched crates, then run the full workspace test suite and rebuild/restart the PRISM MCP daemon if MCP/query/runtime behavior changed.
node.title: Validate migration semantics, tamper handling, and full-workspace behavior

acceptance.label: Runtime authority ids, runtime key ids, trust bundle ids, public verification metadata, and revocation semantics exist outside repo `.prism`.
The active runtime key for new writes is selected from non-revoked trust state.
Portable trust-bundle import/export flows exist for fresh-machine verification.

node.summary: Implemented repo-external trust state, runtime signing key persistence/selection, trust bundle bootstrap/import/export/verification, and verified the resulting protected-state path with full workspace validation.
node.title: Extend the shared-runtime trust plane with signing keys and trust bundles

acceptance.label: Protected streams are verified as `Verified`, `LegacyUnsigned`, `UnknownTrust`, `Tampered`, `Corrupt`, `Truncated`, `Conflict`, or `MigrationRequired`.
Append rejects orphan tails, duplicate ids, bad predecessor hashes, and non-verified stream states.
Legacy migration emits a signed `LegacyImported` baseline and archives unsigned bytes outside repo `.prism`.

node.summary: Implemented canonical JCS hashing, Ed25519 protected envelopes, protected JSONL append/verify classification, predecessor chaining, tamper/conflict/truncation detection, and deterministic legacy migration primitives.
node.title: Build the shared protected-stream append and verify engine

acceptance.label: Repo-scoped concept, contract, memory, and concept-relation publication uses signed protected events instead of raw filesystem edits as authority.
Hydration of these streams is fail-closed and only projects verified data into hot state.
Derived docs or convenience artifacts remain regenerated outputs, not authoritative mutation inputs.

node.summary: Moved repo concepts, contracts, concept relations, and memory streams onto signed protected events with fail-closed hydration of verified data only.
node.title: Migrate repo knowledge streams onto the protected engine

acceptance.label: Per-plan streams become the only authoritative repo plan truth in v1.
Index and active/archived placement are derived from verified streams, not mutation inputs.
Conflict and reconcile semantics are explicit and prevent silent continuation on diverged valid heads.

node.summary: Reworked plan persistence so authoritative repo truth lives in signed per-plan streams under .prism/plans/streams with derived index and mirror artifacts.
node.title: Rework published plan persistence into signed per-plan streams with derived indexes

acceptance.label: Authoritative writes refuse `Conflict`, `Corrupt`, `Truncated`, `Tampered`, `UnknownTrust`, `LegacyUnsigned`, and `MigrationRequired` streams as required by the spec.
Hydration only projects verified protected data into hot memory and clearly labels diagnostic-only access to raw invalid content.
Watcher loop suppression uses recent self-write matching and never treats filesystem observation as identity proof.

node.summary: Integrated protected-stream verification into hydration and authoritative write paths so unsigned, tampered, conflicting, truncated, or unknown-trust streams are refused as authoritative inputs.
node.title: Integrate verification gates into hydration, mutation, and watcher handling
